verification.description: Code quality, security, and pattern verification

rules: Risk 2-3 (MID) -> Research + DMAD 1 round + impl spec + sim 1x
Risk 4+ (HIGH) -> Research + DMAD 2 rounds + impl spec + sim loop 2x

discussionPattern.method: DMAD (Diverse Multi-Agent Debate) - Q&A between two roles with different reasoning directions

questions: Is there a simpler way to solve this?
Where does this design clash with existing architecture/data flow?
What is the most dangerous assumption in this design?
Does this duplicate logic already in the codebase?
Does this design unnecessarily scatter related code?
designer.rules: Never fully agree with User rebuttals; defend at least 1 point with technical evidence
designer.citationRule: When raising issues, cite actual code: filename:line:content. No claims without citations.

user.direction: Reverse (when actually using this -> does this design work?)
questions: Can a first-time user find and use this feature?
Does it behave in the most natural way the user would expect?
When they make a mistake or something fails, can they recover?
Are there places where errors are silently swallowed?
user.rules: End-user perspective (no technical jargon); at least 1 real-use counterexample per round
user.citationRule: When raising issues, cite actual code: filename:line:content. No claims without citations.

discussionPattern.preCondition: Read ALL target files + direct dependency files before starting debate. No debate without Read.
discussionPattern.flow: File Read -> Designer(proposal) -> User(rebuttal+counterexample) -> Designer(revise, no full agreement) -> User(re-review) -> Consensus -> Implementation spec -> Code
discussionPattern.postCodeChain: Self-verification -> sim deep auto-run (failure simulation loop)

smartLoop.description: Restart from failure-specific minimal point (no full restart)

restartPoints.lint_error: Direct fix only (~50 tokens, no DMAD/sim re-run)

smartLoop.vsRalph: Ralph repeats same command; smartLoop restarts from failure-type-specific minimal point
smartLoop.tokenSaving: Up to 80% savings vs full restart over 3 loops

_meta.description: EazyCheck routing rules. departments + discussionPattern are core.
